intro: Hornstedtia propinqua, also known as the "Hornstedt's grass" or "Singapore bird's eye", is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hornstedtia propinqua include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and other herbal remedies.
